strategy: Prefer a forward repair after live durable writes. Before writes, migrations can roll back in reverse order. A full return to Sprint 01 requires restoring a pre-Sprint-02 backup or accepting destructive removal of the Forja schema before reverting code.

tests.name: code rollback target
tests.summary: The exact pre-Sprint-02 main commit passed its complete validation gate in an isolated worktree.

tests.name: immutable version 1 upgrade
tests.summary: Version 1 migration hashes remained byte-identical while version 2 upgraded seeded legacy attempt data.

tests.name: backup and restore round trip
tests.summary: Canonical runs, attempts, events, idempotency receipts, leases, outbox rows, and projection state survived dump and restore verification.

runtime_state_impact: Reverting the code without reconciling the database is unsupported. Preserve a verified backup and either migrate backward before new writes or restore the backup; forward repair is safest once Sprint 02 data exists.
